text.title: ProductionEvaluator — Continuous Quality Monitoring
text.summary: Sampled production evaluation with JSONL persistence, sample rate guidance by traffic volume, and alert analysis prompt pattern.

rule.statement: Set sample rate by traffic: 10% at 1k req/day, 1% at 100k req/day, 0.1% at 1M req/day — keep evaluation cost below 5% of production inference cost.
rule.rationale: Over-sampling wastes budget without improving signal quality; under-sampling misses infrequent failure modes.

rule.statement: Persist evaluation records to a JSONL file or time-series database rather than accumulating in memory.
rule.rationale: In-memory accumulation causes OOM in high-volume long-running processes; JSONL files survive process restarts and can be analyzed historically.

rule.statement: Track benchmark results as a time-series over 4-8 weeks — a single evaluation snapshot has low diagnostic value; trends reveal model drift and quality degradation.
rule.rationale: Point-in-time scores show current quality but not whether it is improving or declining; trend lines are required for meaningful operational decisions.

rule.statement: Calibrate error pattern strings (e.g., "sorry, i cannot", "as an ai") against actual model outputs every 3-6 months.
rule.rationale: Provider phrasings change across model versions; stale patterns produce false negatives that mask real quality issues.

antipattern.description: Running ProductionEvaluator.evaluate_request with random.random() and expecting reproducible debugging of a specific production failure.
antipattern.reason: Non-seeded sampling is not reproducible; the failing request will not reliably be re-captured. Log all raw requests separately from the evaluation sample.

section.title: Alert Analysis
section.reference: Prompt for comparing 24h metrics to 7-day baseline and generating alerts
